notes:
vỡ = to break, shatter, or burst (for solid objects). It’s used when something breaks into pieces, usually glass, dishes, screens, balloons, etc.

Common patterns: làm vỡ + object (you break something) and bị vỡ (something gets broken, often accidentally).  
It can also mean “burst” for things under pressure (bubble, dam) or strong emotions (heart, feelings).  

Close words: gãy (break in two, like a stick or bone), hỏng (broken/not working but not necessarily shattered).

content.sentence: Cái ly ___ khi rơi xuống đất.

translation.en: The glass broke when it fell to the ground.

exampleSentences: Em làm rơi cốc nên nó bị vỡ trên sàn nhà.
Nếu quả bóng đập mạnh vào cửa kính, kính có thể vỡ.
Sau khi nghe tin xấu, anh cảm thấy trái tim mình như vỡ ra.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I dropped the cup so it started to break on the floor.
If the ball hits the glass hard, the glass can break.
After hearing the bad news, he felt like his heart would break.

content.sentence: Cái ly ___ khi rơi xuống đất.

translation.en: The glass broke when it fell to the ground.
